title: crotalaria benguellensis
intro: Crotalaria benguellensis, also known as Benguella bush, is a shrub-like plant native to southern Africa, particularly in Angola and Namibia. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal activities. Its health benefits include treating skin conditions, wounds, and gastrointestinal issues, as well as serving as a natural remedy for fever and pain relief. The plant's herbal preparations often involve using its roots or leaves in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which are then applied topically or ingested to harness its medicinal properties.
